stance: NEUTRAL — High MCS (2 strict beats + 1 in-range hit), post-drawdown technical, growth deceleration
summary: Netflix delivered 2 strict BEATs (actual above HIGH end of guide) and 1 HIT (within range) across 3 independent FY revenue guidance claims since Q3 FY24. Weighted MCS 0.833 (above the 0.50 random baseline but below PLTR/AMD/META/NVDA perfect-track-record cohort). FY25 actual revenue of $45.18B exceeded the original $43-44B framing by 2.7-5.1%, but landed within the raised $44.8-45.2B range. FY26 guidance is $51B (+13% YoY), down from FY25's 16% growth. Operating margins expanded to 30% in FY25 and guide 31.5% for FY26. The stock has corrected -34.8% from the June 30, 2025 high (post 10:1 split) to $87.49 on May 8, 2026, reflecting growth deceleration and 10:1 split absorption. RSI 24.15 signals oversold; death cross active since 2025-12-05.
quotes: We delivered 16% revenue growth, roughly 30% operating profit growth, expanding margins, growing free cash flow, ad sales 2.5x in 2025, we expect that business to roughly double again in 2026 to about $3 billion.
We forecast 2026 revenue at $51 billion, which is up 14% year-on-year.
So as you saw on the letter, we increased our full year revenue guidance to $44.8 [billion] to $45.2 billion.
So we expect to deliver roughly $43 billion to $44 billion of revenue next year based on FX rates at the end of Q3.
So given that, we are maintaining our guidance, our strong outlook for organic growth that we established for 2026.
since 2020, our content amort has grown more than 50%, from under $11 billion to more than $16 billion that we expect to do this year.
bull: Strong MCS — 2 strict BEATs + 1 HIT on 3 independent forecasts

Of the 3 independent FY revenue guidance points: Q3 FY24 INITIAL ($43-44B) and Q4 FY24 REFINED ($43.5-44.5B) were both beat above HIGH end. Q2 FY25 RAISED guide ($44.8-45.2B) was met within the range (HIT). Weighted MCS 0.833 (Beat=1, Hit=0.5).
Operating margin expanded from 28% in FY24 to 30% in FY25; FY26 guidance 31.5% (~50bp y/y). Content amort growth of ~10% in FY26 vs revenue growth of ~14% = continued leverage.
Ads revenue 2.5x in 2025 and management guides ~doubling again in 2026 to ~$3B. Achieved critical scale in all 12 ad-supported countries.
FY25 FCF $8B+ delivered. Q1 FY26 alone generated $5.09B FCF (54.6% FCF margin). Active $10B+ share repurchase program reducing share count.
RSI 24.15 deeply oversold (below 30 = textbook bounce zone). -34.8% drawdown from 52w high creates statistical mean-reversion opportunity. Sell-side cautiously constructive.

2026 slate frontloaded (vs 2025 back-half weighted): Stranger Things final season, Wednesday S2, Knives Out 3, Squid Game S3. Live events (NFL Christmas Day, Mike Tyson). FY26 ad doubling supports ARM expansion.
bear: FY26 guide $51B = +13% YoY, vs FY25 +16%. Q1 FY26 actual +16% YoY but slowing. Mid-teens growth no longer commands hyper-growth multiples.
SMA50 below SMA200 since 2025-12-05; -34.8% drawdown from 52w high $134.12. MACD bearish, ADX 21.5 (developing downtrend). Stock failed to hold $100 support.
Content amort >$16B in 2025, guidance +10% in 2026 to ~$18B. Risk if engagement plateaus and global content ROI compresses — particularly with YouTube/short-form competition.
>50% of revenue international. Q3 FY25 Brazil $619M tax expense one-off. BRL/ARS/TRY/JPY weakness reduces reported revenue growth meaningfully when stripped of FX.
$275M cost carry for M&A activity flagged in Q4 FY25 and Q1 FY26. Potential WBD studio acquisition could impair margins meaningfully and require significant capital outlay.
Stopped reporting subscribers Q1 2025 — buy-side lost the most-used unit-economics anchor. Forces reliance on revenue growth + margin guidance, reducing fundamental transparency.
trigger_up: Q2 FY26 ER (Jul 16, 2026) raises FY26 revenue guide above $51.5B AND OM above 31.5%
Ad revenue tracks above $3B FY26 trajectory (e.g. Q2 FY26 ad rev annualized >$3.5B)
Live sports/event rights expansion (e.g. NBA International, NFL packages) accretive economics
Recovery above SMA200 ($103.34) breaks death-cross structure
Pricing power demonstrated in Q3/Q4 2026 without churn acceleration
trigger_down: Q2 FY26 guide flat or cut to $50B; FY26 OM trimmed below 30%
WBD acquisition confirmed with dilutive financing (>$30B at significant premium)
Ad revenue undershoots $3B FY26 trajectory in Q2 print
Break below 52w low $75.01 — would target $70 area on technical extension
Engagement metrics show YouTube share gains accelerating in core US/UK markets
bottom_line: Netflix is a 'transition' setup: 2 strict BEATs and 1 in-range HIT on 3 independent FY revenue guidance points (weighted MCS 0.833), 16% growth that's normalizing to 13% in FY26, expanding margins to 31.5% target, doubling ad business to ~$3B. RSI 24.15 deeply oversold creates short-term bounce setup; sell-side maintains constructive ratings. Fundamental story intact, multiple has compressed (-52% discount on EV/EBITDA vs peer median). Tactical bias: NEUTRAL with mild bullish tilt — accumulate below $85, scale up on recovery above $100. Key catalyst: Q2 FY26 ER on Jul 16, 2026. NOTE: The previous "perfect 5/5" MCS was methodologically inflated — it counted 5 sequential restatements of the same FY25 target as independent claims, and applied loose scoring (any beat-vs-midpoint counted as BEAT). Corrected to 3 truly independent forecasts using strict BEAT-vs-HIGH-end methodology.
disclaimer: Quoted statements verbatim from NFLX earnings calls. Fundamentals from EDGAR XBRL companyfacts (CIK 0001065280). FactSet 2026-05-12. NFLX completed a 10:1 stock split in late 2025 — pre/post split EPS values are not directly comparable. MCS uses strict scoring: BEAT (actual above HIGH end of guidance range) = 1.0, HIT (within range) = 0.5, MISS (below LOW end) = 0. Only INDEPENDENT new-information forecasts tracked; mere reaffirmations of existing guidance excluded. Not investment advice.
